reasoning: The Indeed seasonally adjusted software development job posting index is currently on a sharp downward trajectory, falling from ~67.9 in January 2025 to 62.59 on March 2, 2025. The trend since early February has been consistently negative at around -0.15 points per day. Because the index uses a 7-day trailing average, abrupt positive spikes are mathematically smoothed out, making a rebound above 62.59 within the next week highly improbable (P=0.12). For horizons of 1 to 3 months, continued sluggishness in tech hiring and the ongoing contraction suggest a continuation of the sub-baseline performance, yielding probabilities of 0.22 (April) and 0.35 (May). As the forecast horizon extends to 6 months (August) and 1 year (March 2026), uncertainty naturally dominates and forces probabilities closer to 0.5. Given the exceptionally low baseline (nearly 40% below pre-pandemic levels), the potential for long-term mean reversion and interest rate easing lends a slight optimistic tilt over a 1-year horizon (P=0.53).
